chunk_a.description: Presses the A button 3 times. Useful for safely chunking through early-game battles or text without risking over-pressing and missing important HP updates.

history.commit_message: Removed autopress_buttons from input_schema as per system instructions.

get_bike_voucher_final.description: Safely gets the Bike Voucher by facing Right, starting the dialogue, and spamming Up+A to ensure YES is selected.
get_bike_voucher_final.python_script:
import json

buttons = ["Right", "A", "A", "A"]
for _ in range(48):
    buttons.extend(["Up", "A"])

print(json.dumps(buttons))

get_voucher_yes.description: Correctly sequences A presses to open the YES/NO prompt, presses Up to select YES, and confirms with A.
get_voucher_yes.python_script:
import json

buttons = [
    "B", "sleep 1000",
    "Right", "sleep 1000",
    "A", "sleep 2000",
    "A", "sleep 2000",
    "A", "sleep 2000",
    "A", "sleep 1000",
    "Up", "sleep 500",
    "A", "sleep 2000"
]
for _ in range(40):
    buttons.extend(["A", "sleep 1000"])
print(json.dumps(buttons))

heal_pokemon_center.description: Presses A repeatedly to navigate the healing dialogue.

history.commit_message: Fixed script to print a JSON array of button strings instead of returning a Python list.
Updated tool description to clarify that it only presses A and does not navigate.
Fixed script to print JSON array.

pace_grass.description: Paces back and forth to trigger encounters. Parametrized to handle any map and axis.

length.description: Number of steps in one direction before turning around.

iterations.description: Number of back-and-forth loops to execute.

pace_grass.python_script:
import json

axis = str(input_data.get('axis', 'horizontal'))
length = int(input_data.get('length', 2))
iterations = int(input_data.get('iterations', 5))

buttons = []
dir1 = 'Right' if axis == 'horizontal' else 'Down'
dir2 = 'Left' if axis == 'horizontal' else 'Up'

for _ in range(iterations):
    for _ in range(length):
        buttons.append(dir1)
    for _ in range(length):
        buttons.append(dir2)

print(json.dumps(buttons[:100]))

get_voucher_safest2.description: Foolproof sequence to select YES for the Fan Club Chairman by alternating A and Up with long delays to bypass slow text rendering and menu delays. Under 100 buttons limit.
get_voucher_safest2.python_script:
import json

buttons = [
    "B", "sleep 1000",
    "Right", "sleep 1000"
]

# Alternate A and Up.
# Large sleep after A ensures text either finishes printing or menu fully opens.
# Up moves cursor to YES safely.
for _ in range(8):
    buttons.extend(["A", "sleep 3000", "Up", "sleep 1000"])

# Mash A to get through the long story
for _ in range(30):
    buttons.extend(["A", "sleep 1500"])

print(json.dumps(buttons))

get_voucher_from_prompt.description: Presses A to open the prompt, Up to select YES, and A to confirm, then mashes A for the story.
get_voucher_from_prompt.python_script:
import json

buttons = [
    "A", "sleep 1500",
    "Up", "sleep 500",
    "A", "sleep 1500"
]

for _ in range(40):
    buttons.extend(["A", "sleep 1000"])

print(json.dumps(buttons))

get_bike_voucher_correct.description: Correctly sequences the exact number of A presses to open the Fan Club Chairman's YES/NO prompt and select YES.
get_bike_voucher_correct.python_script:
import json
buttons = [
    "Right", "sleep 500",
    "A", "sleep 2000",   # 1st A: "Hello, ASH!"
    "A", "sleep 4000",   # 2nd A: "Did you come see me about my POKeMON again?"
    "A", "sleep 1500",   # 3rd A: Opens the YES/NO prompt
    "Up", "sleep 500",   # Moves to YES
    "A", "sleep 1500",   # 4th A: Confirms YES
]
for _ in range(40):
    buttons.extend(["A", "sleep 1500"])
print(json.dumps(buttons))

get_bike_voucher_real.description: Perfect sequence to talk to the Chairman, wait for the prompt to open automatically, select YES, and skip the story.
get_bike_voucher_real.python_script:
import json
buttons = [
    "Right", "sleep 500",
    "A", "sleep 2000",   # Talk -> "Hello, ASH!"
    "A", "sleep 4000",   # Advance -> "Did you come see me about my"
    "A", "sleep 4000",   # Advance -> "POKéMON again?" -> Prompt opens automatically
    "Up", "sleep 1000",  # Move cursor to YES
    "A", "sleep 2000",   # Select YES
]
for _ in range(40):
    buttons.extend(["A", "sleep 1000"]) # Story text
print(json.dumps(buttons))

talk_a.description: Presses A once and waits 2 seconds to safely step through dialogue.
